questions.question: Does WelcomeInvite.tsx render the invite line strictly from the inviteLine prop (rendering nothing when null) rather than a hard-coded fallback string, a TODO placeholder, or an always-true render regardless of prop value? Show the conditional render logic and confirm no default non-null fallback text is baked into the component.

Is WelcomeInvite.tsx pure presentation with zero transport calls, zero card/URL decoding, and zero persistence (saveProfile, add-flow, sendJoinRequest) inside the component itself, matching architecture.md's 'Welcome UI (new)' module boundary and the WelcomeInviteProps seam contract's invariant 'the component itself performs no transport calls, no card/URL decoding, and no persistence'? Confirm via import list -- no import from addDeepLink.ts, contactCard.ts, joinRequestSender.ts, or ProfileContext.tsx's saveProfile.

Could the Welcome UI module (app/src/components/WelcomeInvite.tsx) be deleted and rewritten without modifying any file outside its dependencies_allowed list? If not, which cross-module dependencies exist and do they flow through declared seam contracts in architecture.md?

Given this repo's no-jsdom unit-test convention and the story's own note that WelcomeInvite.tsx has no direct component-level unit test, what is the actual verification evidence for AC-SCREEN-1..3 in this story -- is it limited to code review plus the i18n completeness check (Copy type + en/de both present, function-copy pattern for the dynamic invite lines), with the real behavioral proof deferred to the S3/S4 e2e specs as documented in the story notes? Confirm no false claim of direct unit coverage exists in this story's test files.

Does the pitch section render via useCopy() referencing home.subheadingLead and home.subheadingPoints (not new duplicated welcome.* strings), matching the spec's 'reuse existing home copy so there is a single source of truth' and architecture.md's 'do not duplicate those strings'?

Does WelcomeInvite.tsx expose exactly the props needed for the WelcomeInviteProps contract (inviteLine, nameValue, onNameChange, primaryActionLabel, primaryActionDisabled, onPrimaryAction) without leaking additional caller-specific branching (e.g. an internal 'variant: contact | group' switch that would reintroduce transport-specific logic into the presentation layer)?

Does WelcomeInvite.tsx render <HeroAccents> (the existing component, imported not reimplemented) behind its content, confirmed by a direct import/JSX usage in the component source (evidence: file diff), since decorative accent rendering carries no unit-test assertions by this repo's precedent?

Does WelcomeInvite.tsx render the 'Just chat.' lead and the full five-item value-bullet list (no email / no login-or-password / no phone / no messages from strangers / completely free) sourced from home.subheadingLead / home.subheadingPoints via useCopy(), with no bullet omitted or hardcoded?

Does WelcomeInvite.tsx render exactly one own-name <input> bound to nameValue/onNameChange and exactly one primary action button bound to onPrimaryAction/primaryActionDisabled -- confirm by counting rendered interactive elements in the component source, ruling out a second stray button or a duplicate input.

Does every new welcome.* string appear in both the Copy type and the en and de dictionaries in app/src/lib/i18n.ts, with zero user-visible string literal hardcoded inside WelcomeInvite.tsx (confirm via grep for quoted text used in JSX)?

Do the dynamic invite-line copy entries (e.g. the invited-by-name line, the group-invite line) use the function-copy pattern `(name: string) => string` / `(group: string) => string`, consistent with existing dynamic entries in i18n.ts, rather than string concatenation performed outside the copy dictionary?

Since this story's scope explicitly excludes wiring into app/pages/index.tsx, confirm by diff that no file under app/pages/index.tsx changed in this story, and that AC-RETURN-3 ('first-time visitor at a non-invite route sees today's hero') is therefore trivially satisfied by this story never touching the home page's render path -- is there any accidental import of WelcomeInvite into index.tsx that would violate this?

S2 deliberately did NOT add a welcome.groupPrimaryAction copy key -- the group-variant primary action label is intended to come from the existing copy.groups.joinRequestButton ('Request to Join' / 'Beitritt anfragen') passed in by S4 as the primaryActionLabel prop, documented in architecture.json's notes.group_primary_action_label_decision. Is this consistent with AC-I18N-1's 'no user-visible string is hardcoded in the component' -- since the label text itself lives entirely in i18n.ts either way, does reusing an existing key rather than adding a new welcome.* key still satisfy the AC, or does S4 need to be explicitly briefed on this so it doesn't independently invent a duplicate welcome.groupPrimaryAction key?

WelcomeInvite.tsx uses a label + Input placeholder (copy.welcome.nameLabel / copy.welcome.namePlaceholder) for the name field, whereas the existing JoinRequestCard.tsx uses a label + helper text below the input (no placeholder) instead. Is this a deliberate, acceptable divergence for the new shared component, or should it match JoinRequestCard's established pattern more closely once S4 replaces JoinRequestCard's own name-gate rendering with WelcomeInvite -- i.e. will S4 lose the joinRequestNameHelper / joinRequestNameRequiredHint messaging (the 'enter a name to continue' hint shown only once the button is disabled) since WelcomeInviteProps has no slot for it?

The optional primaryActionLoading prop (defaults to false via `?? false` at the call site) is new surface not explicitly requested by the seam contract in stories.json/architecture.md. Is its addition safe -- i.e. does it stay a strict superset of the required WelcomeInviteProps shape (all other fields exactly as specified, this one purely additive and optional), such that S3/S4 can ignore it entirely without any behavioral difference from a hypothetical version of the component that lacked it?
